messages.content: what did the first portion of Sperling's test show? A. long-term memory is more likely to help the test subjects instantly recall information B. testing affects people's ability to remember C. sensory memory is very sensitive and is quick to forget D. people have no problems remembering lots of letters and numbers
C. sensory memory is very sensitive and is quick to forget

what happens during free association? A. a person talks about a dream, making connections by openly talking B. you explore the role of the brains function in the dream C. you discuss your feelings and analyze your emotions D. a therapist helps you learn empathy for othrs
A. a person talks about a dream, making connections by openly talking

which statement best describes learning? A. you must learn before you can act B. the simplest of actions require learning before they can be carried out C. learning is powerful and can change your life D. all of the above
D. all of the above

carley loves to swim because she feels so fantastic afterward. in fact, she has learned that whenever she wants a rush of natural adrenaline, she needs to swim forty laps in the pool. carley keeps this habit up for years because she likes the outcome she gets each time. what does this demonstrate A. carley will most likely dream about swimming B. carley has become operantly conditioned C. carley opens herself up to modeling behavior D. none of the above
B. carley has become operantly conditioned

how are symbolic and analogical representations similar A. both require thinking B. both involve symbolism C. both are mental images D. both are a basic element of thought E. all are correct except both involving symbolism
E. all are correct except both involving symbolism
according to Freud, what do dreams do A. mask the unconscious mind B. predict the future C. freely explore your conceptions and perceptions D. reveal urges and desires
D. reveal urges and desires

what is common in all cultures about children learning to speak A. children's first sounds are similar in all cultures B. children feel obligated to learn the word for "mother" first of all C. children first learn to use their hands to ask for words D. children listen intently to not repeat grammatical mistakes
A. children's first sounds are similar in all cultures

what is a benefit of observational learning A. watching others can motivate you to try the same actions B. no answer is correct C. you can learn without making mistakes D. you can quit a bad habit automatically
A. watching others can motivate you to try the same actions

the purpose of Bandura's Bobo doll experiment is to demonstrate that children A. will most likely model an adults aggressive actions B. have no preference about which toys they play with C. get mad when they are taken away from their toys D. are not influenced by other people's behavior when engaged in play
A. will most likely model an adult's aggressive actions

terell lives in washington DC where he can see the potomac river from his bedroom window. when he sees this river he thinks of swimming, boating, fun, and friends. what type of mental image is this A. analogical B. symbolic C. representative D. both analogical and symbolism
A. analogical

rachel wants to dance professionally and become good at the Samba. she watches her dance instructor and tries to repeat his leg movements. no matter how hard she tried, rachel moves her legs but they are not the movements the dance instructor demonstrated. did rachel successfully model the dance instructor A. yes because she observed the dance instructor B. no because she was not able to repeat the leg movements exactly C. no because she obviously didnt pay enough attention D. yes because she was motivated to try very hard
B. no because she was not able to repeat the leg movements exactly

how does the view of the twentieth-century scientists differ from ancient cultures when it comes to the meaning of dreams A. ancient cultures understood about REM sleep long before today's scientific discovery B. ancient cultures believed that dreams had spiritual meaning c. scientists know that both religion and biology are involved in dreams D. twentieth-century scientists believe dreams reveal the personal meaning of beliefs
B. ancient cultures believed that dreams had spiritual meaning
_ involves the likelihood of increasing behavior A. positive reinforcement B. positive punishment C. negative punishment D. none of the abovr
A. positive reinforcement

compare and find a similarity between the core-optional and adaptive sleep theories A. both believe that a person sleeps for survival B. both believe that sleeping is necessary for healing C. both believe that a person doesn't need every amount of sleep you get D. no commonalities exist
D. no commonalities exist
true or false, knowing too much information can cause you to forget what you want to remember
True.

what is true about the sleep cycle? select all that apply A. a person can go through the sleep cycle multiple times in one night B. the sleep cycle does not repeat itself in order of 1,2,3,4 and REM C. deep sleep occurs in the first two stages of sleep D. dreams occur during REM stage E. acting out dreams is always a potential danger of REM sleep

A. a person can go through the sleep cycle multiple times in one night
B. the sleep cycle does not repeat itself in order of 1,2,3,4 and REM
D. dreams occur during REM stage

how are morphemes and sentences similar A. both have no limitations for understanding B. both result when a person speaks a phrase out loud C. both are created by more basic partys of language in a hierarchy D. both are created by random combinations
C. both are created by more basic parts of language in a hierarchy

what is the importance of the prefrontal cortex A. it helps with executive functions such as behaving in socially appropriate ways B. it helps you skip steps to get to the end result faster C. it helps you make impulsive decisions to solve a problem without much thought D. none of the above
A. it helps with executive functions such as behaving in socially appropriate ways

what is a practical use of classical conditioning A. dieters can learn their hunger cues and associations with eating B. people can house train an animal C. people can train their dogs with good behavior D. all are examples of using classical conditioning
D. all are examples of using classical conditioning
how can you prevent insomnia A. go to bed sometime before midnight B. dont drink energy drinks or drinks with caffeine before bed C. you cant prevent insomnia D. exercise in the late evenings
B. don't drink energy drinks or drinks with caffeine before bed
how do bees communicate with each other A. by using insect sign language B. through human sign language C. by dancing D. by making extra buzzing noises
C. by dancing

why is sleep deprivation bad for your health A. you often forget your personal responsibilities B. you will develop a taste for energy drinks C. you run the risk of developing a higher body temperature D. you can end up with headaches, dizziness, or depression
D. you can end up with headaches, dizziness, or depression

what does classical conditioning require A. chunking pieces of information together B. independent actions with unrelated results C. chaining small actions to make one big action D. the pairing of two stimuli
D. the pairing of two stimuli

what does the story of HM demonstrate A. the hippocampus only impacts the development of short term memory B. damage to the hippocampus results in amnesia C. people with epilepsy will automatically develop amnesia D. removal of the hippocampus is an effective treatment of epilepsy
B. damage to the hippocampus results in amnesia

what do the experiences of Genie and Victor demonstrate about language acquisition A. adults learn languages better because they have lived together B. language acquisition is not dependent upon age C. people have a critical period of time when they best learn language D. people can fully learn a language for the first time regardless of age
C. people have a critical period of time when they best learn language
true or false, learning is the most often considered a change of behavior that results from experience
True.
what is not a requirement of learning A. using neurons and axons B. communication C. cautiousness D. awareness
C. cautiousness

how can concepts and categories help you write an essay A. they help you develop required symbolism B. concepts and categories help you organize your ideas into paragraphs and sentences C. they will help you create mental images D. concepts and categories help you recover lost memories
B. concepts and categories help you organize your ideas into paragraphs and sentences

what is a similarity between sleep apnea and narcolepsy A. snoring is a characteristic of both B. both conditions increase your energy levels C. both conditions can result in a person feeling tired during the day D. nightmares commonly occur with both conditions
C. both conditions can result in a person feeling tired during the day
during REM sleep our muscles are paralyzed, the activation synthesis theory of dreams says that this temporary paralysis _ the brain A. confuses B. assists C. deceives D. destroys
B. assists

Lori started cello lessons a month ago but has trouble remembering the names of the notes on her sheet music. what can Lori do to learn the note names ? A. she can have a neurologist check to see if she has hippocampus damage B. she can just assume that if she can't learn the notes now she never will C. she can create an acronym to remember the lines of the bass clef D. she can chunk the notes on the music sheet together and play just those
C. she can create an acronym to remember the lines of the bass clef

what does it mean to say that memories are "context-dependent" A. people remember information best if they can use their imaginations B. people remember information in up to seven locations C. people often attach their surroundings to their memories C. context helps people to remember the details of prior events
C. context helps people to remember the details of prior events

what make the restorative and adaptive theories different A. adaptive theories believe that sleeping make you stronger B. adaptive theories discredit evolution C. restorative theories believe that wounds will heal better while you rest D. restorative theories believe in saving energy
D. restorative theories believe in saving energy
a ballet student who learns with the help of his instructor is demonstrating _ learning A. informal B. rote C. cautious D. formal
D. formal
what results when you combine several phonemes together A. a morpheme B. a predicate C. a sentence D. a phrase
A. a morpheme

what is the benefit of deep processing over surface processing A. surface actually has several benefits over deep processing B. surface memory only allows one thought to occupy your mind C. youll have abetter ability to remember information in the future D. you can predict future knowledge
C. you'll have a better ability to remember information in the future

why are nightmares so memorable A. nightmares have a more intense emotional impact B. nightmares reveal personal information C. nightmares encourage the mind to go out of control D. nightmares last longer than regular dreams
A. nightmares have a more intense emotional impact
long-term memory has a _ capacity to hold information A. great B. fleeting C. minimal D. unpredictable
A. great

how are concepts and categories alike A. both require extra reasoning B. categories and concepts are ways to group each other C. a category can exist without any concepts and vice versa D. concepts and categories involve thoughts
D. concepts and categories involve thoughts
